blocks.content: At four seventeen in the morning, Inspector Ray Davenport sat in his car in the parking lot of the Outer Banks Diner and made a phone call.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: It's me. I need to change the filing on the Calloway death. From unexplained to suspicious.
A pause on the other end.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: No, I don't have tox yet. I'm going on — call it thirty years and a conversation I had at one in the morning with a man I just met who noticed something I should have noticed first.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Tell Sheriff Pruett in the morning. And tell him I'm going to need the five people in that house to stay local until further notice. They're not suspects. They're — everyone's a witness until I know different.
He hung up. Sat for a moment looking at the diner's lit sign through the windshield.
He thought about what Julian Crane had said. The quality of the watching.

He had been doing this for three decades. He had worked forty-one homicides, cleared thirty-eight. The three he hadn't cleared still sat in a box in the back of a closet in his house and came out every year or two, when he thought he had enough distance.
He didn't have distance on this one. This one was six hours old.
He went into the diner. Ordered coffee and eggs and sat at the counter and thought.
